question: Which phrase means 'to fail to do what you promised someone'?
She _____ forever talking behind people's backs. (annoying present habit)
Which adjective describes someone who 'refuses to change their mind or consider different viewpoints'?
Read: 'Monks gives Oliver the money that is owed to him and leaves the country. Oliver is adopted by Mr Brownlow.' What is the most important message of the ending?
choices: crime is always rewarded in the end
honesty and kindness eventually triumph over dishonesty
money is the most important thing in life
Oliver should have stayed in the workhouse

question: As a child, I _____ spend my weekends playing with my friends. (setting the scene)
Which phrase means 'to have someone's values, work and lifestyle as your model to follow'?
The person _____ designed the house is a world-famous architect.
Which phrase means 'to talk about someone in an unkind way when they are absent'?
Read: 'A better work-life balance also enables families to spend more quality time together.' What does 'quality time' mean?
choices: time spent working on important projects
time that is enjoyable and meaningful
time that is very short but productive
time spent alone without any distractions

question: Which sentence uses a DEFINING relative clause correctly?
choices: Amman, which is the capital of Jordan, is beautiful.
My brother, who lives in the UK, is a doctor.
The man who gave me the gift is my friend.
Technology, which I find exciting, is always changing.

question: Which phrase means 'a baby that has been recently born into a family'?
choices: a clash between you and a sibling
a newborn baby in the family

question: He _____ go to the park after school every day. (repeated past action)
In which section of an opinion essay should you 'restate your opinion or thesis'?
Produced in South Korea, _____ I-talk has its headquarters, this is a bright new star.
Which phrase means 'to behave foolishly and waste time'?
She is now an _____ figure for everyone in my family.
Read: 'One day, they encouraged Oliver to beg for more food, but this made the managers of the workhouse angry.' Why did the managers get angry?
choices: because the boys were playing games
because Oliver asked for more food, which was against the rules

question: When I was younger, I _____ always stay up late. (annoying repeated habit — past)
Which adjective means 'having a very high opinion of yourself'?
Rewrite: 'I bought my computer at a shop. It has closed down.' (Use relative clause)
choices: The shop where I bought my computer has closed down.
The shop which I bought my computer has closed down.
The shop who I bought my computer has closed down.
The shop whose I bought my computer has closed down.

question: She _____ be tiny, but now she's the tallest in the class. (past state — ended)
Which phrase means 'an extremely positive role model'?
Read: 'People sometimes think that stressful or upsetting events shape us negatively.' Which word in this sentence signals that this is an OPINION?
The year _____ I was born was 2005.
Which phrase means 'to stop being upset and forgive each other after an argument'?
Suha hates anyone who is different from her. She's really _____.
In 'Older people tend to think that teenagers spend too much time relaxing', the phrase 'tend to think' is used to:
choices: generalise about what is usually the case

question: What does 'subsistence' mean in the context of the workhouses?
My friend, _____ I've known for ten years, is very supportive.
Read: 'In conclusion, I would say that while society may have changed, families remain as close as they were, aided by technology.' What is the purpose of this sentence?
choices: to summarise the thesis and main points

question: Which phrase means 'to endure someone or something unpleasant'?
My brother _____ always losing his football boots. It drove my parents crazy.
Which phrase means 'to behave in a way that inspires others'?
Read: 'Oliver was sad to leave the other orphans. Although the boys lived hand-to-mouth, they had a sense of belonging.' What does 'sense of belonging' tell us about the orphans?
choices: they were very wealthy and happy
they felt connected to and accepted by each other despite their poverty
they wanted to leave the orphanage
they were well-fed and cared for

question: The relative pronoun 'whose' is used to show:
Which phrase means 'to suddenly and unexpectedly start crying'?
He _____ always come home late and expect dinner to be ready. (annoying typical past habit)
Which adjective describes someone who 'shows great warmth, charm, and attractiveness in personality'?
That boy, _____ mum is Japanese, is fluent in five languages.
Which phrase means 'to view someone as an inspiration because of their actions'?
